microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end product for Azure's resilience validation, taking it 0→1 from a 2-person operation into a platformized system that sustains 45+ annual drills and drove **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp).

Drove 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, writing API specs and self-service scheduling flows that **reduced operational toil 30%** and shifted execution to a scalable model engineers owned the *what* and *why* of, not just the *how*.

Worked fluidly from 30,000 feet to 3 feet—holding system strategy with leadership while shipping a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new model for continuous validation.

Defaulted to action on unowned problems, directing 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including bridge-leading a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract** spanning compliance-heavy workflows.

Integrated AI into how the team shipped, building an internal **AI agent** and restructuring planning workflows that cut planning cycle time **39%** and increased drill capacity **21%**, prioritizing deliberately instead of running on the last request received.

Said no to low-value requests and reactive feature asks, holding the product shape across four user surfaces (engineering, ops, partners) so the roadmap sequenced by impact rather than by whichever stakeholder escalated most recently.
microsoft_2023: Shipped fast and gathered feedback faster, championing adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows through hands-on user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.

Ran my own user research instead of waiting for a deck—facilitating 11+ interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical gaps, then influenced the roadmap to prioritize **intent-based YAML generation** as the next build.

Prototyped and shipped an **AI-powered semantic search** migration, implementing rigorous metadata standards that cut documentation lookup time **83%** and improved data integrity and retrieval across compliance-heavy internal workflows.

Translated raw user signal into shipped product by patterning interview findings into concrete feature specs, demonstrating the talk-to-a-user-first instinct that changes what gets built rather than refining in isolation.
microsoft_2022: Picked up an unowned prioritization problem and built a unified automation framework across 140+ teams, generating **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launching regions **28% faster** through cross-functional alignment.

Ran discovery with 20+ service teams to quantify **81 hours** of manual effort per region launch, then shaped a roadmap to automate the critical compliance-heavy paths—sequencing what shipped first by operational impact.

Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership the data to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact support, turning a reactive backlog into a deliberately prioritized roadmap.
amazon_robotics: Defaulted to action on a fragile transition, defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations to achieve **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot in a 50-site operational environment.

Measured iteration in days, not sprints—facilitating Agile ceremonies and prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize a developer intake process that ops and engineering both depended on daily.

Held the system shape across IT, Operations, and Engineering, driving alignment to implement automated **CI/CD pipelines** that accelerated the software deployment cycle **25%** and removed friction from a previously manual release path.
pro_painters: Owned the buyer experience end-to-end—managing the full scoping-to-invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals via a new CRM process that increased job bookings **26%** by removing friction at every step of the purchase flow.

Executed a digital-first go-to-market that reduced Customer Acquisition Cost **13%** and lifted conversions **2.7%**, optimizing the website and Google profile to turn passive interest into completed checkout-style transactions.

Ran profitability analysis across 1,000+ project records to surface and seal margin leaks, giving leadership a data-backed pricing model and demonstrating the bias to dig into the numbers rather than read a summary.

tailoring_notes: Leaned hard into Ekho's stated 'who'll thrive' traits: default-to-action, say-no, talk-to-users, ship-fast, 30k-to-3ft, and AI-integrated workflows—each surfaced in at least one bullet.
Mirrored JD vocabulary: 0→1, API specs, prototyping, compliance-heavy workflows, multi-entity permissions, buyer experience, friction, and roadmap ownership of unowned surfaces.
Recast Pro Painters as a buyer-experience / checkout-funnel story to echo Ekho's 'Buy Now' flow and CAC/conversion focus without inventing facts.
Emphasized the AI-agent and semantic-search work to match the founding PM's expectation of prototyping with AI and pushing it to production.
Foregrounded technical-architecture credibility (CI/CD, distributed systems, API specs) to satisfy the BS-in-CS / holds-system-shape requirement.
